categories.name: Picklist/Enum Mapping
categories.description: Differences in how picklist values are stored, sent, and received between HubSpot and Salesforce APIs

categories.name: Deal Stage Mapping
categories.description: Deal pipeline stage mismatches between HubSpot deal stages and Salesforce opportunity stages

categories.name: Record Owner Sync
categories.description: Owner/user assignment differences and the resulting sync loops

categories.name: Duplicate Detection
categories.description: Different matching rules and dedup logic between the two CRMs

categories.name: Currency Format
categories.description: Currency field format differences (ISO code vs symbol, multi-currency handling)

categories.name: ID & Reference Fields
categories.description: Object ID format and reference field differences

mappings.notes: HubSpot API returns picklist values via the `value` field (internal name). Salesforce expects the `label` field (display name). Native connector partially translates known values but fails silently on unmapped ones. Field is set to blank with no error log.
mappings.fixStrategy: Maintain explicit translation table in middleware. Map every HubSpot value to correct Salesforce label. Add blank-field monitoring post-sync.

mappedValues.notes: No Salesforce equivalent — creates mapping error
No Salesforce equivalent — creates mapping error

mappings.notes: HubSpot pipelines and Salesforce opportunity stages rarely map 1:1. Each CRM allows custom stages. Native connector creates a default mapping that often doesn't match actual business process. When stages don't map, sync fails or data is dropped.
mappings.fixStrategy: Map stages explicitly before enabling sync. Test with all pipeline stages. Add fallback stage for unmapped values.

mappedValues.notes: Partial match — HubSpot implies scheduled meeting, Salesforce is prospecting

mappings.notes: Sync loop scenario: HubSpot creates contact with owner A. Sync to Salesforce sets owner B (different user). Salesforce syncs back to HubSpot, updating owner. HubSpot syncs again, creating infinite loop. Native connector lacks loop detection.
mappings.fixStrategy: Set one-directional owner sync. Or add last-sync-source field to detect and block loopback updates.

mappings.notes: HubSpot dedup uses email + custom rules. Salesforce uses its own matching rules. When rules disagree, duplicates are created in one system. Common with contacts where email differs slightly (e.g., john@example.com vs john.doe@example.com).
mappings.fixStrategy: Align matching rules before sync. Use external dedup service (e.g., Insycle) for clean data.

mappings.toolBValue: Amount + Currency ISO code in separate field
mappings.notes: HubSpot stores currency as amount + currency property. Salesforce stores as CurrencyIsoCode on the record + amount field. Multi-currency orgs: both systems must have multi-currency enabled and same ISO codes.
mappings.fixStrategy: Enable multi-currency in both systems. Ensure all ISO codes match.

mappings.notes: IDs are auto-converted by native connector. Custom middleware must handle mapping table: HubSpot numeric IDs to Salesforce 18-char refs. Salesforce 15-char IDs must be converted to 18-char.

errors.errorName: Field Mapping Mismatch — Integration Value vs Label
errors.description: HubSpot sends internal picklist value, Salesforce expects display label. Unmapped values silently blank.
errors.fixSummary: Explicit translation table in middleware + blank-field monitoring

errors.description: HubSpot pipeline stages and Salesforce opportunity stages don't map 1:1, causing sync failures or data loss.
errors.fixSummary: Explicit stage mapping before enabling sync. Test all pipeline stages.

errors.description: Bidirectional owner sync creates infinite update loop between the two CRMs.
errors.fixSummary: One-directional owner sync or add last-sync-source field for loop detection

errors.description: Different dedup rules between HubSpot and Salesforce create duplicate records during sync.
errors.fixSummary: Align matching rules before sync. Use external dedup service.
